executive_bio: Ahmad Ghosn is a doctoral researcher specializing in the geoeconomic structural frameworks of the Middle East, with a specific focus on the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC). His research lies at the intersection of Neoclassical Realism and supply chain resilience, analyzing how geopolitical actors deploy connective infrastructure as mechanisms of statecraft. Prior to his current PhD work, he contributed to extensive intelligence briefings analyzing Eurasian connectivity and systemic risk.
thesis_synthesis: “IMEC functions as a geopolitical operating system requiring structural redundancy to survive Levantine instability.”
